rulings.Case Title: RICHARD BOMONI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 5, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant shifted the burden and Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue regarding whether an assault and battery took place or whether Officer Marcic's actions were objectively unreasonable. Plaintiff relies on his own declaration which contradicts his previous sworn deposition testimony. Such evidence is insufficient to raise a triable issue. Defendant's objections 1, 2, 3, and 3 (sic) to Declaration of Richard Bonomi are sustained. Objection 4 is overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY WHITE et al VS. HANDLERY HOTELS INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Form Interoggs; Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 5, 2013, Line 1. Defendants ANWAR SHAIKH and BENAZIR SHAIKH's Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Responses to Form Interrogatories; Memo of P&A Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED, for the reasons set forth in moving papers and motion is unopposed. Plaintiffs, TERRY WHITE and JOSEPH FERREIRA and their counsel Frank Z. Leidman, Esq. are ordered to provide complete, verified responses, without objection, to the Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Demand for Production of Documents, Set One which were served by counsel for Defendants ANWAR SHAIKH dba "ACER HOTEL" and BENAZIR SHAIKH on February 19, 2013 and to which responses were to be served by TERRY WHITE and JOSEPH FERREIRA on or before March 26, 2013. The complete, verified responses, without objection are due within 15 calendar days of the date of service of the Notice of Entry of Order on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax letter to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt

rulings.Case Title: INGHAM, ERICA ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ALL OTHERS VS. CLUB ONE, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Compliance With Depo Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 5, 2013, Line 27, DEFENDANT CLUB ONE, INC.'s Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoenas. Grant in parted, and denied in part Defendant's motion to compel Kevin Ingham to comply with the document demands attached to the deposition subpoena. Within 2 days, Mr. Ingham shall produce documents, without objection, as to nos. 1-2, 5, 8, 11-12, 15, 18, 21, 22, 23, limited in timeframe to finances for October 2011. Plaintiff has put both her and her husband's finances at issue during this time period by her response to Form Interrogatory no. 212.3. Denied as to nos. 3, 4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 13, 14, 16, 17, 19, 20, 24. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: JOE DRUMMOND et al VS. FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Awarding Attorneys Fees To Pltd'S
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 5, 2013, Line 28, PLAINTIFFS JOE DRUMMOND and RICHARD PERRILLO's Motion For Order Awarding Attorneys Fees To Plaintiffs. Hearing required. The parties are requested to address whether this request for attorney fees can properly be brought as a motion or whether, pursuant to Civil Code section 1365.2(f), it must be considered as part of the damages to be proven at trial. ("A member of an association may bring an action to enforce the member's right to inspect and copy the association records. ¿ A cause of action under this section may be brought in small claims court ¿." ) See Johnson Declaration in Support of Opposition, 7, Ex. E, Complaint, 16 (C). The Court does not have a copy of the Amended Complaint and requests the parties to address whether there is a similar provision in the current Complaint in this action. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ERIK SWENSON et al VS. ROMAN SHADE COMPANY OF SAN MATEO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 5, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT ROMAN SHADE COMPANY OF SAN MATEO, EWM CORPORATION DBA THE ROMAN SHADE COMPANY, MARGARET MINTO and EDGAR MINTO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the general demurrer to the first and second causes of action and the special demurrer for lack of capacity. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the special demurrer for defect of parties. The first and second causes of action are adequately pled. Plaintiffs do not lack capacity to sue. (CCP § 430.10(b).) Plaintiffs admit that there is a defect in parties and request leave to amend to add Chris Wymore as a plaintiff. This request is granted. (CCP § 430.10(d).) The motion to strike is denied because it is procedurally deficient. In the Notice of Motion, Defendants have not complied with Rule of Court 3.1322(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KRISTINE PETTERSON VS. UNITEDHEALTHCARE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 5, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANT UNITEDHEALTHCARE INSURANCE COMPANY, UNITED HEALTH GROUP INC., UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, LORI PUCCI and OPTUM, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration. Granted. Defendants have shown that Plaintiff and Defendants entered into an arbitration agreement. The arbitration agreement covers the disputes at issue in the lawsuit. Defendants demanded arbitration and Plaintiff refused to arbitrate. The agreement is valid, enforceable and not unconscionable. See Dotson v. Amgen, Inc. (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 975; Serpa v. California Surety Investigations, Inc. (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 695; Wilson v. United Health Group, Inc. 2012 WL 6088318 (E.D. Cal. 2012). Plaintiff is ordered to submit her claims to arbitration. The proceedings in this Court are stayed pending completion of arbitration or further order of the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: NORMA GRANADOS VS. METRO MAINTENANCE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 5, 2013, Line 25, DEFENDANT METRO SERVICES GROUP, INC.'s Motion To Stay. The motion is continued to July 11, 2013 on the Court's motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS BLASGEN VS. REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration And Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 5, 2013, Line 26, DEFENDANT REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC's Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration And Stay Action. The petition to compel arbitration is granted, for good cause shown. No opposition was filed. The action now pending in this court (Case CGC-13-530101) is stayed, pending the outcome of the arbitration. The Court has no authority to compel mediation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
